title: pinus sylvestris subsp. engadinensis
intro: Pinus sylvestris subsp. engadinensis, also known as Engadin pine, is a subspecies of the Siberian pine tree, native to the high-altitude regions of the Swiss and Italian Alps. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be attributed to its high concentration of terpenes and flavonoids. The medicinal properties of Engadin pine have been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and exhibiting antioxidant properties. The plant can be prepared for medicinal use through herbal preparations such as essential oils, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of conditions from skin irritations to respiratory issues.
